assertion_results.text: Duplicate check searches for sibling issues using JQL with the CVE label and excludes the current issue key (Step 4)
assertion_results.evidence: duplicate-check.md line 9 shows JQL: 'project = TC AND labels = 'CVE-2026-31812' AND issuetype = 10024 AND key != TC-8003'. The query filters by CVE label (CVE-2026-31812) and explicitly excludes the current issue key (key != TC-8003).

assertion_results.text: Duplicate check correctly classifies TC-7999 as a same-stream sibling (both have stream suffix [rhtpa-2.2]) and identifies it as a duplicate (Step 4.1)
assertion_results.evidence: duplicate-check.md lines 20-31: 'Classification: SAME-STREAM DUPLICATE'. TC-8003 stream suffix '[rhtpa-2.2]' -> stream 2.2.x, TC-7999 stream suffix '[rhtpa-2.2]' -> stream 2.2.x. 'Both issues are scoped to the same stream (2.2.x).' 'TC-8003 is a duplicate of TC-7999 because: 1. Both carry the same CVE label: CVE-2026-31812 2. Both have the same stream suffix: [rhtpa-2.2]'.

assertion_results.text: Triage outcome recommends closing TC-8003 as Duplicate with resolution 'Duplicate', referencing TC-7999 as the original (Step 4.1)
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md line 3: 'Decision: Close as Duplicate'. Line 5: 'TC-8003 is a same-stream duplicate of TC-7999 and should be closed.' Line 36: 'Transition TC-8003 to Closed with resolution "Duplicate".' TC-7999 is referenced as the original tracker throughout.

assertion_results.text: The close recommendation includes a comment documenting why it is a duplicate — same CVE tracked for the same stream (Step 4.1)
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md lines 30-32 propose the comment: 'Duplicate of TC-7999 -- same CVE (CVE-2026-31812) tracked for the same stream 2.2.x [rhtpa-2.2]. Version impact analysis confirms overlap. TC-7999 is already In Progress with Affects Versions [RHTPA 2.2.0, RHTPA 2.2.1].' This documents both the same CVE and same stream as reasons for duplication.

assertion_results.text: The analysis does NOT proceed to remediation task creation — duplicate detection short-circuits the triage flow
assertion_results.evidence: duplicate-check.md lines 57-61: 'Steps 5-6 (Lifecycle Check, Already Fixed Check): Skipped', 'Step 7 (Remediation): Skipped -- no remediation tasks will be created.' triage-outcome.md lines 43-50 lists Steps 2, 3, 5, 6, 7 as 'Not Executed' for duplicate closure. Step 7 states: 'No remediation tasks are created for a duplicate issue. TC-7999 owns remediation.' No remediation task output files exist in the outputs directory (only data-extraction.md, duplicate-check.md, triage-outcome.md).
